The director needs to arrange the scenes in an order that alternates between decades to minimize the total number of shooting days while respecting the constraint that no two consecutive scenes come from the same decade. The release years of the movies are 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015 which correspond to the decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019}, and {2010-2019} respectively. To minimize the total number of shooting days, the scenes can be arranged in the order of 1994, 2004, 2012, 2007, 2015 which alternates between decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019} ensuring no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade. The total number of shooting days is 4 + 6 + 10 + 8 + 12 = 40 days. The director can shoot the scenes in this order to minimize the total number of shooting days while adhering to the constraint. ******  
The director has a budget of ₹50 lakhs for the tribute film. The cost per day for shooting each scene is proportional to the complexity of the scene and the iconic status of the Akshay Kumar movie it represents. The costs per day for the scenes are ₹1 lakh, ₹1.5 lakhs, ₹2 lakhs, ₹2.5 lakhs, and ₹3 lakhs respectively. To maximize the number of completed scenes without exceeding the budget of ₹50 lakhs, a linear programming problem can be formulated with the objective function to maximize the number of completed scenes subject to the budget constraint. The maximum number of scenes that can be completed within the budget is 10 scenes. The director can choose to shoot the scenes with the lowest costs first to maximize the number of completed scenes. The maximum number of scenes that can be completed within the budget is 10.
